name: fix_test262_batch
description: Orchestrates the complete fixing of Test262 tests by coordinating with fix_test262_file to process all JS test files sequentially. Entry point for test conversion workflow.

input.ocaml_test_file: Path to the generated OCaml test file (e.g., tests/tests/Test262_<Feature>.ml)
input.branch: Branch name in test262 repo (e.g., regexp-buffer-boundaries)

phase_1_discovery: Parse the OCaml test file to identify all JS test file references
Extract the mapping between test sections and their original JS sources
Discover all unique JS test files that need processing
List ALL JS files in the relevant test262 directories as backup discovery
phase_2_coordination: For each JS test file found:
- Wait for completion before processing next file
- Handle errors gracefully, continue with remaining files
- CRITICAL: Verify that tests_generated equals assertions_found for each file
phase_3_validation: Run dune build to verify all generated code compiles
Run dune test to verify expect blocks are correct
Count total assertions across all JS files
Count total tests generated in OCaml file
Report summary of: files processed, tests added/fixed, errors encountered

delegation.primary: Delegates individual JS file processing to fix_test262_file
delegation.sequence: Sequential processing (not parallel) to avoid conflicts
delegation.context_passing: Provides js_test_file, ocaml_test_file, test262_repo_path, and branch to each fix_test262_file call
delegation.verification: Must verify each fix_test262_file call returns tests_generated == assertions_found

summary_report.total_js_files: Number of JS source files discovered
summary_report.total_assertions_found: Total assert() calls across all JS files
summary_report.total_tests_generated: Total OCaml tests generated - MUST equal total_assertions_found
summary_report.tests_added_per_file: Detailed breakdown per JS file with assertion count vs test count

constraints: MUST process files sequentially (not in parallel)
MUST wait for each fix_test262_file result before continuing
MUST NOT skip files on error - track and report instead
MUST provide complete context to fix_test262_file for each call
MUST verify tests_generated == assertions_found for each file
MUST run final build and test validation
MUST report discrepancy if total_tests_generated != total_assertions_found
critical_requirements: Every assert() in JS must have a corresponding OCaml test
No test should be skipped due to perceived redundancy
Literal and constructor forms are separate tests
Different flag combinations are separate tests
u mode and v mode tests are both required
